methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $6.0B - $10.0B.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Archer Aviation, a public-stage drone and autonomous systems company, commands a current valuation of $5.0B following $1.5B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment relies on a peer-median approach applied to three same-sector comparables: Joby Aviation ($10.0B, public), Zipline ($7.6B, Series G), and Nuro ($6.0B, Series G).

The peer-median method yields an implied valuation of $7.6B, representing the midpoint across the three comparable companies. The peer set establishes a range of $6.0B to $10.0B, with Joby Aviation occupying the high end as a publicly traded peer and Nuro anchoring the low end. This single triangulation method is the primary quantitative anchor available given limited public comparables in the sector—only one true public peer (Joby) exists in the immediate comp universe, while five private peers provide supplementary valuation reference points.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.5B to $8.7B, centered on the $7.6B peer median. This implies Archer's current $5.0B valuation sits approximately 34% below the median comparable, suggesting either a discount relative to peer growth profiles and near-term revenue catalysts, or a reflection of firm-specific execution risk. Key uncertainties include the lack of public market depth in this sector, the heterogeneity of use cases across autonomous delivery and air taxi verticals, and divergent paths to regulatory approval across peer companies.
